title: The Cabin

jokes.text: David walking in on his parents having sex - 'Oh my go-oh my God!'
jokes.setup: David casually entering his parents' room expecting normalcy
jokes.punchline: The horrified reaction revealing he's witnessed something traumatizing
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom setup with good character-specific horror. David's dramatic reaction sells the cringe effectively.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David! Knock!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We're indisposed!

jokes.text: 'I saw hell is what's happening'
jokes.setup: Parents asking what David saw
jokes.punchline: David's melodramatic description treating it as apocalyptic
jokes.explanation: Perfect David Rose dramatics - treating minor inconvenience as existential crisis with perfect economy.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What did I see? I saw the end of civilization. I saw people I know engaging in behavior that can only be described as... primal. Someone was eating directly from a serving bowl with their hands. With their *hands*. There was no plate involved. No utensil. Just... fingers and dip. It was like watching society collapse in real time.

jokes.text: 'having sex is not just about making children so they can grow up and make you feel bad about having sex'
jokes.setup: Johnny defending his right to intimacy
jokes.punchline: The circular logic of parent-child shame about sexuality
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on the absurdity of generational sexual shame with excellent structure.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm an adult. I have needs. I have a right to my privacy and my dignity.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, no one's denying you that.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, then why do I feel like I'm sneaking around like some sort of teenager?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Because you are sneaking around. You closed the blinds and locked the door at 2 PM on a Tuesday.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Exactly! Because I'm embarrassed! And I wouldn't be embarrassed if you people weren't making me feel ashamed of being a sexual being.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We're not making you feel ashamed. You're making us feel uncomfortable by doing it while we're eating lunch downstairs.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, if you weren't judging me, I wouldn't feel the need to hide it, and if I didn't feel the need to hide it, you wouldn't know about it, and then you wouldn't be uncomfortable!

jokes.text: 'Shame on you for attempting that position at 8 o'clock in the morning'
jokes.setup: David continuing his horrified rant
jokes.punchline: The specific judgment about timing and position
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of David's horror with specific details that make it funnier and more uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: The specific judgment about timing and position

jokes.text: Roland's euphemism 'bump the old uglies'
jokes.setup: Johnny asking for door repairs for privacy
jokes.punchline: Roland's crude interpretation and colorful language
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland vulgarity perfectly in character. The euphemism itself is dated but his delivery makes it work.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, I need you to fix the door. I'd like some privacy.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Privacy? You want me to put a lock on that thing so nobody can get in? Well, that's gonna cost you extra, buddy. And let me tell you, I've seen some things in this town that would make your hair curl. Just last week, I caught Herb trying to—well, never mind. Point is, privacy ain't cheap. But I got just the thing for ya. I'll put in a lock so tight, even you won't be able to get out. Ha!

jokes.text: 'you wanna bring the boat into port and maybe... get your mast scrubbed'
jokes.setup: Roland continuing his sexual metaphors
jokes.punchline: The elaborate nautical sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Roland's extended metaphor shows his character's tendency to over-elaborate crude jokes. Well-structured buildup.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, it's like sailing. You gotta know when to drop your anchor, when to hoist your sails, when to let the wind... fill your... canvas.

jokes.text: 'What're you guys down to now? Like... twice a day?'
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to end the conversation about sex
jokes.punchline: Roland's completely inappropriate personal question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland boundary violation. The casual way he asks such a personal question creates good cringe.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I think we've covered everything there is to know about—

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: So what's your favorite position?

jokes.text: 'Sex life's in the crapper. I just offered him the cabin.'
jokes.setup: Jocelyn arriving and asking how Johnny is
jokes.punchline: Roland immediately oversharing Johnny's private business
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland overshare that immediately creates social awkwardness. Perfectly timed and in character.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: How is Johnny doing?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, he's doing okay. He's been having some stomach issues. I think it's from the stress. He's been very constipated. I gave him some of my fiber supplement, but nothing's really moved yet, if you know what I mean.

jokes.text: 'feel free to use the restraints. We keep 'em under the bed'
jokes.setup: Jocelyn being helpful about the cabin
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning sex toys like they're extra towels
jokes.explanation: Jocelyn's innocent delivery of inappropriate information creates perfect cringe comedy. The casual hospitality tone makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh, and we have some extra items in the bedroom closet if you need them. There are a few massage wands, a rabbit, and some other things that are really just for relaxation.

jokes.text: 'Pick a safe word'
jokes.setup: Already awkward conversation about restraints
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn continuing with more explicit advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and economy. Three words that make the situation infinitely more uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: You know, the key is to make sure you have a safe word. Something you'll remember, but something he won't expect. I use 'pineapple.'

jokes.text: 'I don't want to turn into my parents. Separate beds, separate rooms. Well, separate countries.'
jokes.setup: Moira explaining why they should take the cabin
jokes.punchline: The escalating scale of marital separation ending absurdly
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with perfect Moira dramatic flair. The progression gets more absurd with each step.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We need to take the cabin. Do you know what happens when couples don't have their own space? They start to resent each other. First, it's small things—he leaves his socks on the floor, she rearranges his sock drawer out of spite. Then it escalates: separate bathrooms, separate bedrooms, separate houses, separate continents, and before you know it, you're dividing up the children like they're assets in a hostile takeover, and honestly, at that point, you might as well just go ahead and split the atoms.

jokes.text: 'Am I being punished for something?' / 'David, are you dirty peeping tom?'
jokes.setup: David accidentally interrupting his parents again
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately accusing him of voyeurism
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the opening scene with Moira's characteristic victim-blaming of David.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I didn't realize you were—

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David! Must you always be watching us? It's voyeurism!

jokes.text: If you tell me that you're pregnant, I'm gonna vomit on this floor right now
jokes.setup: Parents announcing they have 'very important news'
jokes.punchline: David's horrified assumption and graphic threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect character reaction with good structure and economy. David's dramatic response is very in-character.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We have some very important news.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, we do.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you're getting divorced.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What? No.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're both dying.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: No, David.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh, thank God. Because if you were both dying, I would kill myself.

jokes.text: 'Didn't you once take the wrong baby home from preschool?' / 'Alexis looked Chinese as an infant.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of parental responsibility
jokes.punchline: Moira's casual racism as a defense mechanism
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing Moira's oblivious prejudice while deflecting from actual negligence. Character-specific comedy.

jokes.text: Alexis looked Chinese as an infant
jokes.setup: Defending herself against the wrong baby accusation
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre justification for the mix-up
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy. Perfectly captures Moira's oblivious privilege and dramatic justifications.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I was wearing my reading glasses. The babies all look the same when you're not wearing your reading glasses.

jokes.text: Roland's elaborate directions to the cabin with multiple turns and landmarks
jokes.setup: Johnny asking for simple directions
jokes.punchline: Roland's unnecessarily complex verbal map
jokes.explanation: Setup for later payoff when directions are wrong. Roland's tendency to over-complicate simple things.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, I just need to get to the store. Can you give me simple directions?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh sure, bud. So you're gonna go down Main Street, but not all the way down Main Street, you're gonna hang a left at the old Kabin, you know, before it got all fancy, then you're gonna see a tree, or maybe it was there last week, could've been cut down, but if it's there, you're gonna go right, and if it's not there, you're still gonna go right, because that's where the store is, but sometimes it moves around depending on inventory, so you might wanna call ahead.

jokes.text: 'It was so clean. It was really clean.' / 'because you had a maid to keep it very clean for you.'
jokes.setup: David claiming he kept his apartment clean
jokes.punchline: Alexis pointing out he had hired help
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic showing David's self-delusion about his self-reliance and Alexis deflating it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I kept my apartment immaculate.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You had a housekeeper come twice a week.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, and I kept it clean between visits.

jokes.text: 'No. I kept it clean after Cecilia cleaned.'
jokes.setup: David being called out about his maid
jokes.punchline: His absurd logic about cleaning what's already clean
jokes.explanation: Perfect David logic - defending himself with increasingly absurd reasoning that reveals his privilege.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, if I don't clean what's already clean, how will I know it's still clean?

jokes.text: 'I am looking forward to sleeping in a bed that his not... made for a toddler.'
jokes.setup: David explaining why he's looking forward to space
jokes.punchline: The pathetic reality of his current sleeping situation
jokes.explanation: Good detail showing the family's reduced circumstances through David's perspective.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm looking forward to space. It's the only place where I won't have to share a bed with Patrick.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: We share a bed?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Every night. You steal all the covers and you hog the pillow.

jokes.text: 'I'm out of mask.'
jokes.setup: David's plan to relax with a face mask
jokes.punchline: The simple practical limitation stopping his self-care routine
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character work. Four words that capture David's vanity and current limitations.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm going to treat myself to a facial mask tonight. I need to decompress.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: That's wonderful, darling. Which one are you using?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: The one I ordered from that spa in Switzerland. It's supposed to be incredibly restorative.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: How long do you leave it on?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Forty-five minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And how will you answer the door if a client calls?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: ...I'll reschedule them.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: With what excuse? 'I'm sorry, I can't meet with you, I'm wearing a mud mask'?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes. Actually, that's exactly what I would say.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, there's your relaxation ruined.

jokes.text: 'you do have an interest in lounging on a bed that mom and dad have had sex all over?'
jokes.setup: David rejecting Alexis's party idea
jokes.punchline: Alexis making their shared bed disgusting to think about
jokes.explanation: Good callback that weaponizes the earlier awkwardness to manipulate David into agreeing.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, if you're not going to let me throw a party, then I'm going to make this room so disgusting that you'll have no choice but to let me move out.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: How is that going to work?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm going to start eating crackers in bed.

jokes.text: 'I miss being surrounded by loose acquaintances who think that I'm... funny, and smart, and charming.'
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining what she misses about her old life
jokes.punchline: Her honest admission about preferring shallow relationships
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - Alexis being accidentally profound about the superficiality she misses.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I just miss having people around me who actually cared about what I had to say, you know? People who valued my opinions and my thoughts.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: But honestly? I also kind of miss the shallow relationships where nobody actually knew me well enough to judge me for my mistakes.

jokes.text: 'Any more and game play gets too yelly. It gets very yelly.'
jokes.setup: David setting rules for the party size
jokes.punchline: His made-up word and serious concern about volume
jokes.explanation: Good David-specific anxiety about chaos. 'Yelly' is a perfect made-up word that sounds like him.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No more than eight people. It's a Friendsgiving, not a Friendsgivings.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And everyone has to be gone by 9 PM. I can't have people 'round-abouting' in my living room at all hours.

jokes.text: 'Key's under the mat, is it? The key is under the mat! Agh! That's great! Lying son-of-a...'
jokes.setup: Johnny confidently following Roland's directions
jokes.punchline: His mounting frustration as the directions prove wrong
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Roland's overcomplicated directions. Johnny's escalating anger is well-structured.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, yes, I've got it. Left at the intersection, then straight for about two miles.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, no, I'm perfectly fine. I don't need to stop and ask for directions.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I know exactly where I'm going.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, this should be it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: This is... this is not it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland said left at the intersection. This is clearly not the intersection.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm going back. I'm going back to find the intersection.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Where is the intersection? There's no intersection here!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I am never taking directions from Roland Schitt again. Never.

jokes.text: Key's under the mat, is it? The key is under the mat! Agh! That's great! Lying son-of-a... Agh! Idiot man! Moron!
jokes.setup: Johnny finding out the key isn't where Roland said it would be
jokes.punchline: His mounting frustration and angry ranting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of frustration. Works as physical comedy though not particularly original.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland said the key would be under the mat. It's not under the mat. It's not under the planter. It's not under the rock shaped like a sleeping dog, which, by the way, is just a regular rock. Where is this key?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I have been standing in the rain for fifteen minutes looking for a key that apparently doesn't exist, which is very on-brand for this town and very on-brand for Roland.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: So now I'm wet, I'm frustrated, and I have a social disease from touching that rock.

jokes.text: The porn scenario beginning with 'Well hellooooo. Oh, my car broke down and it's raining'
jokes.setup: Johnny and Moira arriving at what they think is their cabin
jokes.punchline: The clichéd porn dialogue playing in the background
jokes.explanation: Standard porn parody setup. Works as background joke but isn't particularly original.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, here we are. Our cabin.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's... quaint.

dialogue.speaker: TV Voice
dialogue.line: Oh yes, that's it. Right there. Oh my God, yes!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What is that?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I believe the previous tenants left their... entertainment on.

dialogue.speaker: TV Voice
dialogue.line: You like that, don't you? You dirty girl!

jokes.text: 'Oh, all those types of people move away from here.' / 'That's funny.' / 'No, I'm serious.'
jokes.setup: David asking for smart, funny people for games night
jokes.punchline: Stevie's matter-of-fact assessment of local talent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan delivery with David's misunderstanding making it funnier. Great structure.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I want to invite smart, funny people. People who are engaging and culturally aware.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, that eliminates basically everyone in this town.

jokes.text: 'He finished high school.' / 'I don't love his look.'
jokes.setup: Stevie suggesting Eric as qualified
jokes.punchline: David immediately dismissing him for superficial reasons
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing David's shallow priorities despite claiming to want intellectual company.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Eric seems qualified.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Eric? No. Absolutely not.

jokes.text: 'looks are important? Oh... Why don't we throw a pageant?'
jokes.setup: David's admission that looks matter for games night
jokes.punchline: Stevie's sarcastic suggestion to formalize his shallowness
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcasm from Stevie calling out David's ridiculous standards with a perfect escalation.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Looks matter for games night.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Oh, well then we should probably just make it official and start ranking people by their appearance at the door.

jokes.text: 'Trust me, if I had time, we would, but we don't, so...'
jokes.setup: Stevie's sarcastic pageant suggestion
jokes.punchline: David seriously considering it as if it's a legitimate option
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - completely missing sarcasm and treating an absurd suggestion as reasonable. Excellent character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You should enter a pageant.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: A pageant? I... that's actually not the worst idea you've ever had.

jokes.text: 'She get it?' / 'Get what?' / 'Like "get it" get it. It, like the vibe.'
jokes.setup: David asking about Sarah as a potential guest
jokes.punchline: His inability to clearly articulate his pretentious standards
jokes.explanation: Good David-ism where he can't explain what he wants but knows it's important. The repetition shows his struggle.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, she's not... she's very talented, but she's not really the caliber of guest that we're looking for.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: What caliber is that?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, you know, someone who's... accomplished. Refined. Someone with a certain... je ne sais quoi.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Sarah has all of those things.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, but not in the way that I need.

jokes.text: Can I be on your team? You sound really fun.
jokes.setup: David explaining games need to run a certain way
jokes.punchline: Stevie's sarcastic enthusiasm for his controlling nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie sarcasm. Her deadpan delivery makes the mockery more effective.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Games need to run a certain way. There are rules.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Oh wow, David Rose needs things to be a certain way? That's so surprising. I had no idea you were controlling.

jokes.text: I was half expecting early unibomber
jokes.setup: Moira commenting on the rustic cabin
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic comparison to a domestic terrorist's hideout
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira character moment with her dramatic, specific cultural references delivered casually.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This place looks like a domestic terrorist's hideout... if the terrorist had given up and decided to just live here.

jokes.text: 'Roland has gay friends! Again, shocked and delighted.'
jokes.setup: Seeing what appears to be a gay couple in a photo
jokes.punchline: Moira's theatrical surprise at Roland's supposed tolerance
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Moira's earlier phrase with her assumptions about Roland's prejudices.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Roland! Is that you with another man? I had no idea you were so... progressive.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: What? No, that's my brother!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, that's even more progressive.

jokes.text: 'I think that's his wife.' / 'No, they're both called husband.'
jokes.setup: Johnny correcting Moira about the photo
jokes.punchline: Moira's strange logic about how gay relationships work
jokes.explanation: Funny misunderstanding showing Moira's odd logic about relationships while trying to be progressive.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's not a photo of us together.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, it should be. You're always together.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's... that's not how photos work.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, in gay relationships, you're supposed to be joined at the hip. I assumed you'd also be joined at the camera.

jokes.text: they just don't know any better
jokes.setup: Talking about why the people in the photo look happy
jokes.punchline: Johnny's condescending assumption that ignorance equals bliss
jokes.explanation: Shows Johnny's privileged perspective, though the observation itself isn't particularly original.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, they look happy because they don't know what's about to happen to them. Ignorance is bliss.

jokes.text: 'She used to be my babysitter.' / 'Great. Wow. Okay.'
jokes.setup: David asking how Eric knows Stevie
jokes.punchline: Learning their age difference makes this awkward
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment revealing the social dynamics David doesn't understand. His response shows his discomfort.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: How do you know Stevie?

dialogue.speaker: Eric
dialogue.line: Oh, we met in high school.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: High school? Stevie went to high school here?

dialogue.speaker: Eric
dialogue.line: Yeah, I was a senior when she was a freshman.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You were a senior when she was a freshman? So you're like... significantly older than her.

dialogue.speaker: Eric
dialogue.line: I mean, not that significantly.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Eric, that's deeply inappropriate.

jokes.text: 'there's a 50/50 chance there's meat in it'
jokes.setup: Twyla bringing pie from work
jokes.punchline: The completely random uncertainty about a basic ingredient
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail that suggests the café's questionable food practices while being innocently delivered.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: I brought pie from the cafe. It's, uh, it's blueberry. Or at least that's what they told me.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Though, honestly, I'm not entirely sure what went into it. One of the new guys was making the filling, and I didn't want to ask too many questions.

jokes.text: 'I don't think I can fit ten names on this piece of paper.'
jokes.setup: David explaining the game rules
jokes.punchline: Eric's literal misunderstanding of the instructions
jokes.explanation: Shows Eric's lack of sophistication that David was worried about. Simple but effective character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Okay, so the rules are simple. You have to think of a number between one and a hundred, and I have to guess it.

dialogue.speaker: Eric
dialogue.line: Oh! I thought you meant I had to think of a number and then you would guess what I was thinking before I thought of it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's not... that's not how that works.

dialogue.speaker: Eric
dialogue.line: Well, that would be impressive.

jokes.text: 'Can you put yourself down?'
jokes.setup: Eric still confused about the game
jokes.punchline: His innocent question that could be interpreted darkly
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in innocent question. Works on multiple levels - game confusion and accidental darkness.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So you still don't understand the rules?

dialogue.speaker: Eric
dialogue.line: I'm just trying to figure out where everyone puts their balls.

jokes.text: 'Who the... Bleep... is this?' / 'He's the worst, okay? So just breathe through it.'
jokes.setup: Unwanted party guests arriving
jokes.punchline: David's immediate hatred and Alexis's casual warning
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic with David's instant judgment and Alexis treating terrible people as minor inconveniences.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, they're here.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Who is 'they'? And why do they seem intent on ruining my evening?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Just... be nice. They're not that bad.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, I have formed a very strong opinion about these people, and I have formed it quickly.

jokes.text: No worries. We're here for the party. Keg's in the truck
jokes.setup: David trying to maintain his structured games night
jokes.punchline: Dane's casual revelation that he's brought party supplies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing David's worst fears coming true about the event getting out of control.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Games night is a structured event with specific rules and etiquette.

dialogue.speaker: Dane
dialogue.line: Oh, I brought some party supplies to make it fun.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Party supplies? This isn't a party, it's games night.

dialogue.speaker: Dane
dialogue.line: Yeah, I know. I brought streamers and a disco ball.

jokes.text: 'I can't see out of one eye. But I'm still alive, that's the main thing.'
jokes.setup: Eric appearing to be injured
jokes.punchline: His cheerful attitude about significant injury
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy with Eric's positive spin on injury. Good contrast between content and delivery.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my god, what happened to you?

dialogue.speaker: Eric
dialogue.line: I fell down the stairs at the store.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Are you okay? Do you need to go to the hospital?

dialogue.speaker: Eric
dialogue.line: No, I'm fine! It's just a little bump. Nothing to worry about!

jokes.text: 'this is our cabin.' / 'Oh, no, no, no. This is Roland and Jocelyn's cabin.' / 'No, they're a mile up the road.'
jokes.setup: Johnny's confidence about being in the right place
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Roland's directions were completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the directions setup. The awkwardness of being in strangers' cabin adds to the comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland assured me we were going the right way. He said 'You can't miss it.'

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, we've been driving for three hours. I'm pretty sure we missed it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: He was very confident about his directions.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Johnny, we're in a completely different county. We're not even in the right province anymore.

jokes.text: 'You can't say mother! You can't say mother!' / 'She wasn't getting it.' / 'That's the point!'
jokes.setup: Alexis breaking charades rules
jokes.punchline: David's rule-obsessed frustration with her casual cheating
jokes.explanation: Good David perfectionism versus Alexis's casual rule-breaking. Shows their different approaches to games.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, you can't just act out the entire movie! There are rules.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, David, it's just charades.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Just charades? JUST charades? There are very specific guidelines that exist for a reason. You're supposed to use gestures and pantomime, not dialogue.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I was doing an accent.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: An accent is not a gesture, Alexis.

jokes.text: 'Every time the vein in my brother's eye twitches, we take a drink.'
jokes.setup: Suggesting drinking games
jokes.punchline: Targeting David's stress-induced physical tic
jokes.explanation: Mean but funny sibling cruelty. Alexis turning David's stress into entertainment is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: We could play a drinking game where we take a shot every time David does that thing with his face.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What thing with my face?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That stressed out thing. Like this—

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You'd be drunk in like five seconds.

jokes.text: 'I'm sorry that I wanted to have fun tonight and invite a few random guys over and try and make out with them, okay? You're not the only one dying in this town.'
jokes.setup: Alexis defending her party choices
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic comparison of boredom to death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis dramatics. Treating social boredom as literal death while casually admitting to wanting random hookups.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, boring is literally like death. It's the same thing. You're just sitting there, existing, and nothing is happening, and you're dying inside. So really, I'm protecting everyone by throwing a party.

jokes.text: 'So his eye twitched five times, so drink up, bitches!'
jokes.setup: The drinking game based on David's stress
jokes.punchline: Counting David's anxiety as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Callback to Alexis's cruel joke. Shows the party guests' cruelty but isn't particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, we could always play a drinking game based on David's stress levels.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's not a game, that's a cry for help.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No, no, no. Every time David gets anxious, we take a shot.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We'd all be dead by dinner.

jokes.text: 'we didn't realize that, uh, strangers would already be in the cabin when we locked it.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why the door is locked
jokes.punchline: The logical explanation that makes the whole situation more awkward
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan explanation of the absurd situation. Polite way of calling them intruders.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: The door is locked because I locked it.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: I was trying to have a private moment.

jokes.text: 'believe it or not, we are in the middle of a very intense game of drunken charades and my team is losing. Badly.'
jokes.setup: Stevie trying to convince David to return
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact delivery of absurd competitive stakes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan treating ridiculous situation seriously. The competitive aspect is perfectly delivered.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: David, you have to come back. The motel is falling apart without you. I'm serious. I found a family of raccoons living in the ice machine, and I named them after your family members because I was so lonely.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Also, I've been competing with the Holiday Inn down the road, and if I don't beat them by next quarter, I'm going to have to eat a tire.

jokes.text: 'It's not my thing either, but you were right; We're way too similar and I am having a very hard time losing'
jokes.setup: Stevie explaining why she needs David's help
jokes.punchline: Admitting their shared competitive neuroses
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier dialogue while revealing Stevie's competitive side that matches David's.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I need your help with something.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Of course. What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm competing in this thing and I need to win. I can't lose.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Okay, well you've come to the right person because I also cannot lose at anything. It's a compulsion.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I know.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So we're perfect for each other then.

jokes.text: 'Maybe there's um... some other way you can pay me, if you get my drift.'
jokes.setup: Moira saying she can't pay the handyman
jokes.punchline: Johnny's attempt at porn-style dialogue
jokes.explanation: Callback to the porn they saw earlier. Clichéd but works for showing their attempt to recreate fantasy.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, well, well... looks like someone's got a leaky pipe.

jokes.text: 'Well, maybe I'll use a bigger screw, Mrs. Rose.'
jokes.setup: Continuing the handyman roleplay
jokes.punchline: The obvious sexual double entendre about tools
jokes.explanation: Very obvious sexual pun. Works for the cringe factor but lacks originality or cleverness.

tmdb_overview: Looking for privacy, Johnny and Moira take the Mayor up on his offer of using his cabin for a night, while Alexis and David use the opportunity to throw a party.
insight: Cringe escalation drives a middle-tier episode; 53 jokes land at modest 2.09-per-minute pace.
